description_long: This radio receiver features a robust, rectangular metal housing with a military green finish and two prominent black antennas on top. The front panel is equipped with several interactive controls, including a large central dial, toggle switches, and labeled ports for audio connections. The front also displays a frequency indicator and volume control, with clear white labeling for each function. The rear side is mostly solid green with a QR code and four recessed feet, while the opposite side features a black, perforated grille likely serving as a speaker or ventilation. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for field or urban deployment, with a utilitarian design focused on functionality. The object is compact and portable, with a hard, matte surface and minimal decorative elements.
description: A compact, military green radio receiver with dual antennas, a control panel with dials and switches, and a perforated grille on one side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with dials, switches, labeled ports, and a central antenna.
description_view_1: Top/rear view displaying the two antennas, a solid green surface, and a visible QR code.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the black perforated grille, likely for sound output or ventilation, and a single antenna.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain green surface and two antennas, no visible controls or grilles.
